verdict_reasoning: IZUMO4 lacks a transmembrane domain (confirmed by multiple sources) and is predicted to be a soluble, intra-acrosomal protein of sperm. It is lost after the acrosome reaction, consistent with intra-acrosomal localization rather than plasma-membrane display. NCBI notes nuclear localization; HPA notes cytoplasmic expression in seminiferous duct cells. No evidence of stable PM anchoring, TM-partner tethering, or cell-state-induced outer-leaflet display. No therapeutic programs targeting it on the cell surface. The protein body is not accessible from the extracellular face.
